chapter_title.en: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein

text: 1225 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ وَقْتِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ سَاعَةَ تَزُولُ اَلشَّمْسُ إِلَى أَنْ تَمْضِيَ سَاعَةٌ فَحَافِظْ عَلَيْهَا فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ «لاَ يَسْأَلُ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَبْدٌ فِيهَا خَيْراً إِلاَّ أَعْطَاهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1225 - Imam Abu Ja'far (as) said:
"The beginning time for the Jumu’ah prayer is when the sun declines (zawal) until an hour has passed. Maintain it carefully, for the Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
'No servant asks Allah (swt) for good at this time except that He grants it.'"
